text.title: enterprise-xr-applications Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for enterprise-xr-applications as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enterprise device approval can take 6-12 months. Build into plans.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always verify SDK capabilities against current vendor docs (visionOS, Quest Build, MRTK3 versions move fast).

failure-mode.description: Auto-generated training flows skip recovery paths.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents must be explicitly prompted to handle "user got lost" / "user retried 3x" branches.

failure-mode.description: ROI claims drift to the unmeasurable.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require leading + lagging indicators with measurement method.

failure-mode.description: Procurement language matters — agent should produce VPAT requirements and data-residency clauses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Correct procurement language is required for enterprise IT approval.

failure-mode.description: Agent recommends single-vendor lock-in for new pilots.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: "Long-session comfort" is hardware-specific — agent must check FOV, weight, IPD range.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pancake-lens vs Fresnel makes a huge difference in comfort.

failure-mode.description: Multi-user collab in XR can violate confidentiality if voice/spatial audio leaks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent must flag E2E encryption requirements.
